GUMBINER IN SUPPORT OF MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS REPLY TO DEFENDANT RICHARD TOMS OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF RICHARD TOM AND FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 57739138) FILED BY PLAINTIFF HRD COFFEE SHOP INC. YEUNG, DAVID

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS HRD COFFEE SHOP INC. AND DAVID YEUNG'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF RICHARD TOM AND FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER - 1. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED, WITH THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT TOM TO PROCEED ON THE NEXT AVAILABLE DATE TO COUNSEL AND THE PARTIES. 2. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER ALSO IS GRANTED, WITH PLAINTIFF YEUNG TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION ON THE FIRST AVAILABLE DATE AFTER THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT TOM IS COMPLETED. 3. DEFENDANT TOM'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/EG

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT RICHARD TOM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20-DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH TRADE LIABLES AS TO GOODS AND SERVICES ONLY AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S TRADE LIBEL CLAIM FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW BECAUSE THE ALLEGEDLY LIBELOUS STATEMENTS DO NOT CONCERN PLAINTIFFS' SERVICES OR PRODUCTS. ALL OTHER ARGUMENTS MADE IN SUPPORT OF THE DEMURRER LACK MERIT. EXCEPT FOR THE TRADE LIBEL CAUSE OF ACTION, ALL OF THE CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED PER GOVERNING LAW. THERE IS NO OBLIGATION FOR A PLEADER TO ATTACH A WRITTEN CONTRACT TO STATE A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF A WRITTEN CONTRACT. FAILURE TO PAY IDENTIFIABLE PROFITS SUPPORTS A CLAIM FOR CONVERSION. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT REQUIRED TO PLEAD SPECIAL DAMAGES FOR THEIR DEFAMATION CLAIM. WHILE CIVIL CONSPIRACY IS NOT AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM, BUT RATHER A BASIS TO BE HELD LIABLE FOR WRONGFUL CONDUCT, THE CIVIL CONSPIRACY ALLEGATIONS ARE OTHERWISE WELL PLED AND THERE IS NO PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT BY THOSE ALLEGATIONS BEING LABELED A SEPARATE CLAIM. THE PREVAILING IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

Off Calendar Jan-15-2016 Continued To Apr-06-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gordon McAuley of Williams & Gumbiner LLP telephone number 415-755-1880 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff HRD Coffe Shop.
John Jennings of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman Scheley telephone number 650-989-5400 for Defendant Richard Tom.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT RICHARD TOM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 20-days leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action if Plaintiff can allege in good faith trade liables as to goods and services only and overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiff's trade libel claim fails as a matter of law because the allegedly libelous statements do not concern plaintiffs' services or products. All other arguments made in support of the demurrer lack merit. Except for the trade libel cause of action, all of the claims are adequately pled per governing law. There is no obligation for a pleader to attach a written contract to state a claim for breach of a written contract. Failure to pay identifiable profits supports a claim for conversion. Plaintiffs are not required to plead special damages for their defamation claim. While civil conspiracy is not an independent claim, but rather a basis to be held liable for wrongful conduct, the civil conspiracy allegations are otherwise well pled and there is no prejudice to defendant by those allegations being labeled a separate claim. The prevailing is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

Notice Of Preliminary Injunction And Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jul-31-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOEL P. GUMBINER, ESQ. of Williams & Gumbiner LLP, Tel No. 415-755-1880 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. PAMELA B. BUMATAY, ESQ. of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman Scheley APLC, Tel No. 650-989-5400 appearing for Defendant Richard Tom;
Atty. ANDREW H. DAI, ESQ. of the Law Offices of Darius T. Chan APC, Tel No. 415-398-8308 appearing for Defendant Joanna Banks.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court vacated its tentative ruling and ordered as follows: PLAINTIFFS HRD COFFEE SHOP INC. AND DAVID YEUNG'S Motion for Preliminary Injunction is granted. Defendant shall not sell or in anyway attempt to sell the name HRD or HRD Smoke & Grill and/or the name, logo, awning, recipes, and goodwill of HRD Coffee Shop. This includes any physical item containing the name or letters HRD. Counsel have met and confered on the language of the Court's order, and Plaintiffs' counsel shall submit a proposed order tracking that approved language. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
